physicalForm=`spray` matched the template aerosol form.
Task text or workplace setting matched the template context.

Preserved contextOfUse=`worker-tier2-bridge` and sourceModule=`exposure-scenario-mcp` for downstream ART-side review.

screeningHandoffSummary.workerRoutingNextStep: Collect task-control and ventilation inputs for an ART- or Stoffenmanager-aligned adapter.

assumptions.name: use_amount_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product amount per event was supplied in milliliters and converted with density.

assumptions.name: density_g_per_ml

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP heuristic density screening defaults

assumptions.rationale: Default density used for volume-to-mass conversion in screening mode, resolved from product category, physical form, and product subtype when available.

assumptions.name: product_mass_g_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Converted volume-based use amount into mass using density.

assumptions.name: concentration_fraction
assumptions.rationale: Chemical fraction in the product was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: use_events_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Use frequency was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: chemical_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product mass per event multiplied by concentration fraction.

assumptions.name: aerosolized_fraction

source.title: RIVM Disinfectant Products Fact Sheet trigger-spray airborne-fraction default for surface sprays

assumptions.rationale: Aerosolized fraction defaulted from application_method='trigger_spray' and product_category='disinfectant'.

assumptions.name: room_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Room volume was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: air_exchange_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Air exchange rate was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: exposure_duration_hours
assumptions.rationale: Exposure duration was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: inhalation_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Inhalation rate was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: body_weight_kg
assumptions.rationale: Body weight was supplied explicitly for dose normalization.

assumptions.name: source_distance_m
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 source distance was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: spray_duration_seconds
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 active spray duration was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: near_field_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 near-field volume was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: airflow_directionality
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 airflow directionality class was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: particle_size_regime
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 particle-size regime was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: released_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Released mass = chemical mass per event x aerosolized fraction.

assumptions.name: spray_duration_hours
assumptions.rationale: Converted Tier 1 spray duration from seconds into hours.

assumptions.name: far_field_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Far-field volume = room volume minus near-field volume.

assumptions.name: near_field_exchange_turnover_per_hour

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP Tier 1 NF/FF screening parameter pack

assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 screening turnover resolved from the packaged airflow-directionality profile.

assumptions.name: particle_persistence_factor

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP Tier 1 NF/FF screening parameter pack

assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 persistence factor resolved from the packaged particle-regime profile.

assumptions.name: interzonal_mixing_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Near-field volume multiplied by the Tier 1 screening exchange turnover plus ventilation.

assumptions.name: far_field_average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Far-field room average derived from the Tier 0 room model over the full exposure window.

assumptions.name: near_field_active_spray_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Active spray breathing-zone concentration derived from a screening near-field increment added to the far-field room average.

assumptions.name: breathing_zone_time_weighted_average_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Time-weighted breathing-zone average over the full event duration.

assumptions.name: inhaled_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Inhaled mass per event combines active-spray near-field and remaining far-field periods.

assumptions.name: inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Daily inhaled mass equals inhaled mass per event multiplied by events/day.

assumptions.name: normalized_external_dose_mg_per_kg_day
assumptions.rationale: Normalized external dose = inhaled mass per day / body weight.

assumptions.name: worker_control_factor

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP worker ART-side execution surrogate

assumptions.rationale: Worker control factor was supplied as an execution override.

assumptions.name: respiratory_protection_factor

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP worker ART-side execution surrogate

assumptions.rationale: Respiratory protection factor was supplied as an execution override.

assumptions.name: adjusted_inhaled_mass_mg_per_day

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP worker ART-side execution surrogate

assumptions.rationale: Adjusted inhaled mass per day was derived from the baseline inhaled mass, worker control factor, and respiratory-protection factor.

assumptions.name: normalized_worker_inhaled_dose_mg_per_kg_day

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP worker ART-side execution surrogate

assumptions.rationale: Normalized worker inhaled dose was derived from the adjusted inhaled mass and body weight.

qualityFlags.message: Worker Tier 2 adapter request was ingested and normalized into an ART-side intake envelope.
Tier 1 screening geometry and spray hints were preserved as ART-side context only; they are not treated as solved occupational determinants.
Matched packaged ART determinant template `janitorial_disinfectant_trigger_spray_v1` with aligned status.
Default value for 'density_g_per_ml' comes from a heuristic screening source rather than a curated factor pack.

The current spray scenario uses a broad product_category without a product_subtype. Provide a narrower subtype such as `indoor_surface_insecticide`, `crack_and_crevice_insecticide`, `air_space_insecticide`, or `surface_trigger_spray_disinfectant` to unlock more specific ConsExpo-aligned screening branches.
Tier 1 inhalation resolves a screening near-field/far-field split, but remains a deterministic external-dose model rather than a calibrated aerosol simulator.
No packaged Tier 1 profile matched the current product family and application method; screening geometry and regime inputs remain fully caller-defined.
Population profile indicates worker or occupational context; the scenario should be interpreted as worker-task triage, not as a consumer-use result.
Worker-tagged scenario uses the current shared screening engine rather than a dedicated occupational adapter. Inspect worker routing guidance before using the output downstream.
Worker inhalation surrogate execution completed with explicit control and respiratory-protection modifiers.

limitations.message: This ingest step does not execute ART, simulate workplace concentrations, or produce a compliance-ready occupational estimate.
Generic Direct-Use Exposure MCP task fields are mapped into an ART-aligned intake envelope using bounded, reviewable heuristics.
Tier 1 screening hints remain contextual surrogates and should not be confused with measured workplace determinants.
Tier 1 NF/FF uses a deterministic screening exchange-rate mapping from airflow_directionality rather than measured interzonal airflow.
Particle-size effects use a coarse screening persistence factor and do not model droplet evaporation, deposition, or full aerosol dynamics.

This worker-tagged scenario reuses direct-use screening semantics and does not represent ECETOC TRA, ART, Stoffenmanager, measured workplace monitoring, or a regulatory occupational compliance determination.
The current execution path is an ART-aligned surrogate layered on top of existing screening inhalation kernels; it is not a real ART solver.
Worker control and respiratory-protection effects are applied as bounded heuristic factors rather than determinant-specific model terms.

notes: Execution reuses the existing inhalation screening kernels and then applies bounded worker control and respiratory-protection modifiers.
The current output is an ART-aligned surrogate screening estimate, not a real ART run or occupational compliance result.

fitForPurpose.label: worker_inhalation_control_aware_screening
suitable_for: worker inhalation screening with explicit control modifiers
PBPK-ready external inhalation dose handoff after expert review

tierSemantics.tier_rationale: Worker inhalation execution preserves the strongest available underlying screening kernel and layers explicit control and respiratory-protection factors on top.
required_caveats: Interpret the result as a worker control-aware screening estimate rather than a validated occupational model output.
Keep the determinant template match, quality flags, and limitations attached to any downstream use.
forbidden_interpretations: Do not treat the result as a real ART or Stoffenmanager execution.
Do not treat the respiratory-protection factor as a compliance-assured protection factor.
Do not treat the output as a final occupational risk conclusion.

executedValidationChecks.title: Worker inhalation external dose vs benchmark `worker_inhalation_janitorial_trigger_spray_execution`
executedValidationChecks.note: Compares the current result against the packaged worker inhalation benchmark `worker_inhalation_janitorial_trigger_spray_execution` with a +/-5% acceptance band.

executedValidationChecks.title: Worker inhalation baseline dose vs benchmark `worker_inhalation_janitorial_trigger_spray_execution`
executedValidationChecks.note: Checks the preserved screening baseline against the packaged worker inhalation benchmark `worker_inhalation_janitorial_trigger_spray_execution`.

executedValidationChecks.title: Worker inhalation control-adjusted concentration vs benchmark `worker_inhalation_janitorial_trigger_spray_execution`
executedValidationChecks.note: Checks the control-adjusted breathing-zone surrogate against the packaged worker inhalation benchmark `worker_inhalation_janitorial_trigger_spray_execution`.

notes: Worker inhalation validation is currently benchmark-regressed against packaged surrogate execution cases, not an external occupational solver.
A source-backed occupational biocidal spray/foam study is attached when the task family matches disinfectant or pest-control trigger spraying.
A narrow executable external concentration check is available for a study-like small-scale handheld BAC trigger-spray benchmark.
Executable checks run only when the task matches one of the governed worker benchmark patterns.

interpretationNotes: The baseline concentration and dose come from the strongest available inhalation screening kernel preserved by the worker Tier 2 path.
Worker control and respiratory-protection modifiers are then applied transparently as bounded factors.
When the task is vapor-generating and no direct spray kernel applies, execution falls back to a labeled room-average vapor-release surrogate.
